<p><b>Sankarankovil</b> is a town in the <a href="/wiki/Tenkasi_district" title="Tenkasi district">Tenkasi district</a> in the Indian state of <a href="/wiki/Tamil_Nadu" title="Tamil Nadu">Tamil Nadu</a>. It is located on the eastern slopes of <a href="/wiki/Western_Ghats" title="Western Ghats">Western Ghats</a>. The town houses the <a href="/wiki/Sankara_Narayanasamy_Temple" title="Sankara Narayanasamy Temple">Sankara Narayanasamy Temple</a>. Sankarankovil is also 3rd is largest town in Tenkasi district.
</p>
<meta property="mw:PageProp/toc">
<div class="mw-heading mw-heading2"><h2 id="History">History</h2><span class="mw-editsection"><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">[</span><a href="/w/index.php?title=Sankarankovil&action=edit&section=1" title="Edit section: History"><span>edit</span></a><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">]</span></span></div>
<p>Sankarankovil is home to the Sankara Narayanar Temple, which was built by Ugra Pandiyan in 900 BC. Houses the deity by the name Sankara Narayanan, which is half Shiva and the other half Vishnu. According to folklore, the devotees of <a href="/wiki/Hari" title="Hari">Hari</a> (<a href="/wiki/Vishnu" title="Vishnu">Vishnu</a>) and <a href="/wiki/Shiva" title="Shiva">Shiva</a> once quarreled with each other to determine which god was more powerful, until Shiva appeared as Sankaranarayanar to show his devotees that Hari and Shiva were one and the same. Thus, it is held sacred by <a href="/wiki/Saivites" class="mw-redirect" title="Saivites">Saivites</a> and those Hindus who believe that Shiva and Vishnu are a single deity. Srivaishnavites of Tamil Nadu who worship Vishnu only do not accept this and hence reject the temple. The deities of the temple are Sankareswarar, <a href="/wiki/Gomathi_Amman" title="Gomathi Amman">Gomathi Amman</a> and Sankaranarayanar. Sankarankovil is also said to be home to the deity named Avudai Ambal.
</p><p>In older times, the city was called as <a href="/wiki/Sankaranayinarkoil" title="Sankaranayinarkoil">Sankaranayinarkoil</a>. It is also noted for the "Adi Thabasu" festival.
</p><p>There is a belief that the sand from the temple, known as <i>puttrumann</i> (<i>puttru</i> meaning ant hill, <i>mann</i> meaning sand) is capable of curing all diseases. Devotees believe that Sankarankovil's <i>Nagachunai</i> (sacred tank) was dug by serpent kings named Paduman and Sangam, and has a miraculous power to heal those who bath there. Once, a <a href="/wiki/Deva_(Hinduism)" title="Deva (Hinduism)">deva</a> called Manikkeerivan was cursed by the goddess <a href="/wiki/Parvathi" class="mw-redirect" title="Parvathi">Parvathi</a> and, as a result, had to come to earth and work as a gardener in a beautiful garden. One day while he was clearing a snake pit, the snake's tail was also cut. He found a <a href="/wiki/Lingam" title="Lingam">lingam</a> next to the snake. He went and informed his king, Ugra Pandiyan. The king considered it to be the god's wish to stay there and constructed the temple and a city around it.
</p>
<figure class="mw-default-size mw-halign-right" typeof="mw:File/Thumb"><a href="/wiki/File:Skoil1.jpg" class="mw-file-description"><img src="//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/b/b7/Skoil1.jpg" decoding="async" width="240" height="320" class="mw-file-element" data-file-width="240" data-file-height="320"></a><figcaption>Sankaranarayanar koil, Sankarankoil, Tenkasi district</figcaption></figure>
<div class="mw-heading mw-heading2"><h2 id="Demographics">Demographics</h2><span class="mw-editsection"><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">[</span><a href="/w/index.php?title=Sankarankovil&action=edit&section=2" title="Edit section: Demographics"><span>edit</span></a><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">]</span></span></div>
<p>According to <a href="/wiki/2011_census_of_India" title="2011 census of India">2011 census</a>, Sankarankoil had a population of 70, 574 with a sex-ratio of 1,012 females for every 1,000 males, much above the national average of 929.<sup id="cite_ref-dashboard_2-0" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-dashboard-2"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>2<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup> A total of 5,660 were under the age of six, constituting 2,867 males and 2,793 females. <a href="/wiki/Scheduled_Castes_and_Scheduled_Tribes" title="Scheduled Castes and Scheduled Tribes">Scheduled Castes and Scheduled Tribes</a> accounted for 22.47% and .3% of the population respectively. The average literacy of the town was 74.9%, compared to the national average of 72.99%.<sup id="cite_ref-dashboard_2-1" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-dashboard-2"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>2<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup> The town had a total of 14536 households. There were a total of 24,064 workers, comprising 493 cultivators, 2,003 main agricultural labourers, 2,245 in house hold industries, 17,190 other workers, 2,133 marginal workers, 34 marginal cultivators, 247 marginal agricultural labourers, 413 marginal workers in household industries and 1,439 other marginal workers.<sup id="cite_ref-2011census_3-0" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-2011census-3"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>3<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup> Hindus are the majority in the town with 90% of the population, followed by Islam (5%), and Christianity (5%).
</p>
<div class="mw-heading mw-heading2"><h2 id="Politics">Politics</h2><span class="mw-editsection"><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">[</span><a href="/w/index.php?title=Sankarankovil&action=edit&section=3" title="Edit section: Politics"><span>edit</span></a><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">]</span></span></div>
<p><a href="/wiki/Sankarankoil_Assembly_constituency" title="Sankarankoil Assembly constituency">Sankarankovil Assembly constituency</a> is part of <a href="/wiki/Tenkasi_Lok_Sabha_constituency" title="Tenkasi Lok Sabha constituency">Tenkasi Lok Sabha constituency</a>.<sup id="cite_ref-4" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-4"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>4<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup> E. Raja from DMK is the MLA.
</p>
<div class="mw-heading mw-heading2"><h2 id="Economy">Economy</h2><span class="mw-editsection"><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">[</span><a href="/w/index.php?title=Sankarankovil&action=edit&section=4" title="Edit section: Economy"><span>edit</span></a><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">]</span></span></div>
<p>Apart from <a href="/wiki/Agribusiness" title="Agribusiness">agribusiness</a>, the town is known for its textiles. Nearly 4000 powerlooms are directly involved in producing various kinds of textile products such as terry towels, cotton saris, <a href="/wiki/Lungi" title="Lungi">lungis</a>, handkerchiefs, etc. The produced products are marketed all over India directly.
</p>

<div class="mw-heading mw-heading3"><h3 id="Air">Air</h3><span class="mw-editsection"><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">[</span><a href="/w/index.php?title=Sankarankovil&action=edit&section=6" title="Edit section: Air"><span>edit</span></a><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">]</span></span></div>
<p><a href="/wiki/Tuticorin_Airport" class="mw-redirect" title="Tuticorin Airport">Tuticorin Airport</a> is the nearest airport located 95.4 km (59.3 mi) from Sankarankovil. <a href="/wiki/SpiceJet" title="SpiceJet">SpiceJet</a> serves Tuticorin with two flights from <a href="/wiki/Chennai" title="Chennai">Chennai</a>.
</p><p>The nearest international airports are <a href="/wiki/Madurai" title="Madurai">Madurai</a> (120km) and <a href="/wiki/Thiruvananthapuram" title="Thiruvananthapuram">Thiruvananthapuram</a> (148 kms.) away from Sankarankovil. These airports connect with Chennai, Sri Lanka, Singapore and other major cities of India. 
</p>
<div class="mw-heading mw-heading3"><h3 id="Road">Road</h3><span class="mw-editsection"><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">[</span><a href="/w/index.php?title=Sankarankovil&action=edit&section=7" title="Edit section: Road"><span>edit</span></a><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">]</span></span></div>
<p>Sankarankovil has two bus stands. The main bus stand is near the Sankarankovil Municipality office, and is also called the Anna Bus Stand. The new bus stand is nearby to the Sankarankovil Taluk office, and is also called the Thanthai Periyar New Bus Stand.
</p><p>Sankarankovil is situated at the intersection of State Highways 41 and 76.
</p><p>The main bus routes are to cities / towns like <a href="/wiki/Tirunelveli" title="Tirunelveli">Tirunelveli</a>,<a href="/wiki/Virudhunagar" title="Virudhunagar">Virudhunagar</a>, <a href="/wiki/Madurai" title="Madurai">Madurai</a>, <a href="/wiki/Tuticorin" class="mw-redirect" title="Tuticorin">Tuticorin</a>, <a href="/wiki/Nagercoil" title="Nagercoil">Nagercoil</a>, <a href="/wiki/Tenkasi" title="Tenkasi">Tenkasi</a>, <a href="/wiki/Rajapalayam" title="Rajapalayam">Rajapalayam</a>,<a href="/wiki/Aryankavu" title="Aryankavu">Aryankavu</a> <a href="/wiki/Srivilliputhur" title="Srivilliputhur">Srivilliputhur</a>, <a href="/wiki/Sivakasi" title="Sivakasi">Sivakasi</a>, <a href="/wiki/Surandai" title="Surandai">Surandai</a>, <a href="/wiki/Kalugumalai" title="Kalugumalai">Kalugumalai</a>, <a href="/wiki/Thiruvenkatam" class="mw-redirect" title="Thiruvenkatam">Thiruvenkatam</a>, <a href="/wiki/Valliyur" title="Valliyur">Valliyur</a>, <a href="/wiki/Courtallam" title="Courtallam">Courtallam</a>, <a href="/wiki/Shencottah" class="mw-redirect" title="Shencottah">Shencottah</a>, <a href="/wiki/Theni" title="Theni">Theni</a>,<a href="/wiki/Kumili" title="Kumili">Kumili</a>,<a href="/wiki/Papanasam,_Tirunelveli" title="Papanasam, Tirunelveli">Papanasam, Tirunelveli</a>, <a href="/wiki/Ambasamudram" title="Ambasamudram">Ambasamudram</a>, <a href="/wiki/Kanyakumari" title="Kanyakumari">Kanyakumari</a>, <a href="/wiki/Tiruchendur" title="Tiruchendur">Tiruchendur</a>, <a href="/wiki/Rameshwaram" class="mw-redirect" title="Rameshwaram">Rameshwaram</a>, <a href="/wiki/Tirumangalam,_Madurai" title="Tirumangalam, Madurai">Tirumangalam</a>, <a href="/wiki/Aruppukottai" class="mw-redirect" title="Aruppukottai">Aruppukottai</a>, <a href="/wiki/Kovilpatti" title="Kovilpatti">Kovilpatti</a>, <a href="/wiki/Sivagiri,_Tirunelveli" class="mw-redirect" title="Sivagiri, Tirunelveli">Sivagiri</a>, <a href="/wiki/Kayathar" class="mw-redirect" title="Kayathar">Kayathar</a>, <a href="/wiki/Sattur" title="Sattur">Sattur</a>, <a href="/wiki/Nanguneri" title="Nanguneri">Nanguneri</a>, <a href="/wiki/Ettaiyapuram" class="mw-redirect" title="Ettaiyapuram">Ettaiyapuram</a>, Karivalamvandha Nallur and so on. Sankarankovil has state transport (SETC) bus routes to <a href="/wiki/Kodaikanal" title="Kodaikanal">Ooty</a>, <a href="/wiki/Munnar" title="Munnar">Munnar</a>, <a href="/wiki/Madurai" title="Madurai">Madurai</a>, <a href="/wiki/Chennai" title="Chennai">Chennai</a>, <a href="/wiki/Coimbatore" title="Coimbatore">Coimbatore</a>, <a href="/wiki/Trichy" class="mw-redirect" title="Trichy">Trichy</a>, <a href="/wiki/Palakkad" title="Palakkad">Palakkad</a>, <a href="/wiki/Calicut" class="mw-redirect" title="Calicut">Calicut</a>, <a href="/wiki/Dindigul" title="Dindigul">Dindigul</a>, <a href="/wiki/Hosur" title="Hosur">Hosur</a> and so on.
</p>
<div class="mw-heading mw-heading3"><h3 id="Rail">Rail</h3><span class="mw-editsection"><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">[</span><a href="/w/index.php?title=Sankarankovil&action=edit&section=8" title="Edit section: Rail"><span>edit</span></a><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">]</span></span></div>
<p>The Sankarankovil Railway Station (Code: SNKL) is a major railhead in Tenkasi district. All the trains running in Virudhunagar - Sengottai line makes a halt at this station. 
</p><p>A Daily Thrice Passenger train that runs between <a href="/wiki/Madurai" title="Madurai">Madurai</a> and <a href="/wiki/Sengottai" title="Sengottai">Sengottai</a> on both directions makes a technical halt at Sankarankovil. 
</p><p>Expresses and superfast expresses including <a href="/wiki/Pothigai_Express" title="Pothigai Express">Pothigai Super fast express</a>, <a href="/wiki/Kollam" title="Kollam">Kollam</a> Express Connects Sankarankovil to <a href="/wiki/Chennai_Egmore_railway_station" title="Chennai Egmore railway station">Chennai Egmore</a> via <a href="/wiki/Virudhunagar_Junction_railway_station" title="Virudhunagar Junction railway station">Virudhunagar</a>, <a href="/wiki/Madurai_Junction_railway_station" title="Madurai Junction railway station">Madurai</a>, <a href="/wiki/Dindigul_Junction_railway_station" title="Dindigul Junction railway station">Dindigul</a>, <a href="/wiki/Tiruchchirappalli_Junction_railway_station" title="Tiruchchirappalli Junction railway station">Tiruchirapalli</a>, <a href="/wiki/Villupuram_Junction_railway_station" title="Villupuram Junction railway station">Villupuram</a>. 
</p><p><a href="/wiki/Silambu_Express" title="Silambu Express">Silambu Super fast Express</a> from <a href="/wiki/Sengottai_railway_station" title="Sengottai railway station">Sengottai</a> also connects <a href="/wiki/Chennai_Egmore_railway_station" title="Chennai Egmore railway station">Chennai Egmore</a> but via different route. Via <a href="/wiki/Virudhunagar_Junction_railway_station" title="Virudhunagar Junction railway station">Virudhunagar</a>, <a href="/wiki/Manamadurai_Junction_railway_station" title="Manamadurai Junction railway station">Manamadurai</a>, <a href="/wiki/Karaikkudi_Junction_railway_station" title="Karaikkudi Junction railway station">Karaikudi</a>, <a href="/wiki/Tiruchchirappalli_Junction_railway_station" title="Tiruchchirappalli Junction railway station">Tiruchirapalli</a>.
</p>
<div class="mw-heading mw-heading2"><h2 id="Municipality">Municipality</h2><span class="mw-editsection"><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">[</span><a href="/w/index.php?title=Sankarankovil&action=edit&section=9" title="Edit section: Municipality"><span>edit</span></a><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">]</span></span></div>
<p>Sankarankovil includes many localities, including Karivalamvandha Nallur, Gomathi Nagar, Gomathiyapuram Nagar, Ramasamiapuram, Sangupuram Nagar, Lakshmiyapuram Nagar, Vadukkupudhur, Therkku Sankarankovil, NGO Colony, Avrmv Colony, Agasthiyar Nagar, Ezhil Nagar, and Mullai Nagar.
</p>
<div class="mw-heading mw-heading2"><h2 id="Sankarankovil_RTO">Sankarankovil RTO</h2><span class="mw-editsection"><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">[</span><a href="/w/index.php?title=Sankarankovil&action=edit&section=10" title="Edit section: Sankarankovil RTO"><span>edit</span></a><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">]</span></span></div>
<p>Sankarankovil RTO was opened by chief minister Ms. <a href="/wiki/J._Jayalalithaa" title="J. Jayalalithaa">J. Jayalalithaa</a>  via video conference.
</p>
<div class="mw-heading mw-heading2"><h2 id="Notable_people_from_Sankarankovil">Notable people from Sankarankovil</h2><span class="mw-editsection"><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">[</span><a href="/w/index.php?title=Sankarankovil&action=edit&section=11" title="Edit section: Notable people from Sankarankovil"><span>edit</span></a><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">]</span></span></div>
<ul><li><a href="/wiki/Devaneya_Pavanar" title="Devaneya Pavanar">Devaneya Pavanar</a> (Tamil writer)</li>
<li><a href="/wiki/Vaiko" title="Vaiko">Vaiko</a> (founder of <a href="/wiki/Marumalarchi_Dravida_Munnetra_Kazhagam" title="Marumalarchi Dravida Munnetra Kazhagam">MDMK</a>)</li>
<li><a href="/wiki/Karuppasamy" class="mw-redirect" title="Karuppasamy">Karuppasamy</a> (minister in <a href="/wiki/ADMK" class="mw-redirect" title="ADMK">ADMK</a> government, up to 22 October 2011)</li>
<li><a href="/wiki/S._Thangavelu" title="S. Thangavelu">S. Thangavelu</a> (ex state minister, member of parliament (R.S.) [D.M.K]</li>
<li><a href="/wiki/Vivek_(actor)" title="Vivek (actor)">Vivek</a> (Actor)</li>
<li><a href="/wiki/S._J._Suryah" title="S. J. Suryah">S. J. Surya</a> (Actor)</li></ul>
